evidence_quotes.quote: laws that would defend customers rights from publishers destroying their games and media after they bought the game or licence.
take away right of access and/or terminate support after customers have bought the game or media and that media becomes unusable.

paragraphs.text: Hi there. It's really nice to see important steps like these in regards of Online Safety in European media spaces. However, I'd like to suggest an additional step that could benefit from this initiative. This step would be to set forth laws that would defend customers rights from publishers destroying their games and media after they bought the game or licence. It's sadly a recurring phenomenon, that media companies and game publishers take away right of access and/or terminate support after customers have bought the game or media and that media becomes unusable. A great initiative that tackles this situation is "Stop Killing Games". Besides that initiative, it would also be great to see if the Digital Fairness Act could also put forth safety methods that would protect the rights of European consumers in this way aswell. I hope this step could be added into the existing steps aswell.
